message:
feat(mvcc): add deterministic synthetic workload evaluation for writer routing

Adds a full synthetic-workload evaluation framework on top of
the Track E5.2 writer-routing decision function. The framework
lets the routing contract be evaluated against three canonical
workload shapes (disjoint pages, overlapping pages, hot page
contention) under three placement profiles (baseline unpinned,
home-lane pinned, home-node pinned), and compares a
stable-hash baseline against the `decide_writer_routing_target`
decision function on the same deterministic input.

All of this lives in `writer_routing_telemetry.rs` and is
purely additive — no changes to the existing decision
function, no new runtime behavior, no changes to consumers of
the existing types.

## New public enums

- `WriterRoutingMode { BaselineStableHash, ConflictTopologyHints }`
  — which routing strategy is being evaluated on a given run.
- `WriterRoutingSyntheticWorkload { DisjointPages,
  OverlappingPages, HotPageContention }` — the three canonical
  shapes the framework exercises.
- `WriterRoutingPlacementProfile { BaselineUnpinned,
  HomeLanePinned, HomeNodePinned }` — the three home-hint
  profiles tested.

All three enums have `const fn as_str(self) -> &'static str`
helpers returning snake_case labels so the enum values can be
round-tripped through telemetry / JSON / CLI output
deterministically.

## New config type

`WriterRoutingSyntheticConfig` captures every knob a run
needs: `scenario_id`, `workload`, `placement_profile`,
`lane_count`, `iterations`, `writers_per_iteration`. Defaults
to `scenario_id="writer_routing.synthetic"`,
`workload=OverlappingPages`,
`placement_profile=BaselineUnpinned`, and a small
lane/iteration footprint suitable for tests.

## New summary / comparison types

- `WriterRoutingSyntheticSummary` — per-run summary: total
  writers, total runtime nanos, per-writer latency vector,
  conflict / retry / fallback counters, remote-ownership
  events, publication retries, visibility handoff time,
  stale-snapshot rejects, and a
  `WriterRoutingSyntheticFairnessSummary` with per-lane
  assignment counts plus the max-minus-min spread for load
  balance evaluation.
- `WriterRoutingSyntheticFairnessSummary` — fairness breakout
  kept separate from the rest of the summary so callers that
  only care about fairness can pull that slice cheaply.
- `WriterRoutingSyntheticComparison` — pairs a baseline and a
  routed `WriterRoutingSyntheticSummary` and exposes helpers
  for the deltas that downstream dashboards care about
  (`retry_rate_delta`, `publication_retry_rate_delta`, etc.).

## New public entry points

- `evaluate_writer_routing_synthetic_workload(config,
  routing_mode, decision_config) -> WriterRoutingSyntheticSummary`
  — runs one deterministic synthetic workload for one routing
  mode. Pure function: same inputs always produce the same
  summary, so CI can lock in numeric thresholds.
- `compare_writer_routing_synthetic_workload(config,
  decision_config) -> WriterRoutingSyntheticComparison` —
  convenience wrapper that runs the same workload twice
  (once with `BaselineStableHash`, once with
  `ConflictTopologyHints`) and returns the paired result.

## Internal helpers

A set of private helpers keeps the evaluation loop
deterministic and allocation-friendly:

- `synthetic_txn_token(raw)` — builds a `TxnToken` from a
  session index.
- `synthetic_workload_pages(workload, writer_index,
  natural_lane_index)` — the deterministic page set for this
  writer under the chosen workload shape.
- `synthetic_shared_pages(workload, writer_index)` — the
  deterministic set of shared pages this writer touches, used
  to construct conflict history.
- `synthetic_home_hint(placement_profile, lanes,
  page_owners, pages, natural_lane, planned_commit_seq)` —
  builds the appropriate `WriterHomeHint` for the placement
  profile under test.
- `synthetic_baseline_lane(lanes, session_id)` — stable hash
  fallback.
- `synthetic_lane_node(...)` — lane→node projection.
- `synthetic_wait_nanos(workload)` — fixed-shape wait model.
- `evaluate_synthetic_selection(workload, pages,
  selected_lane, page_owners)` — turns a lane selection into
  a `SyntheticOperationOutcome` (latency, conflicts, retries,
  fallback, remote-ownership events, publication retries,
  visibility-handoff time, stale-snapshot rejects).
- `update_synthetic_lane_snapshot(lane, txn_token, pages,
  shared_pages, outcome)` — writes the per-iteration outcome
  back into the per-lane snapshot so the *next* writer sees
  it.
- `push_unique_page_capped` / `push_unique_txnid_capped` /
  `push_unique_txn_token_capped` — dedup helpers that respect
  the SmallVec inline cap for each lane snapshot field.
- `percentile_u64(values, p)` — integer-quantile helper used
  by the latency summary.

## Imports

`std::collections::HashMap` is added at the top of the file
for `page_owners: HashMap<PageNumber, SyntheticPageOwner>`,
and `TxnEpoch` joins the existing `CommitSeq, PageNumber,
TxnId, TxnToken` import from `fsqlite_types`.

## Why this lives in the telemetry module

The evaluation framework is the *input test harness* for the
decision function, not a new telemetry surface of its own.
Keeping it next to both the input types (Track E5.1) and the
decision function (Track E5.2) means a future agent editing
any of the three surfaces has a single file to reason about,
and the synthetic-workload types can share the same SmallVec
capacities and deterministic ID assignments without
cross-file coupling.
